logistics.explanation: Includes complimentary two-night stay at Four Seasons Hotel Miami on Brickell Ave and full police escort maintaining minimum 18 mph pace from start to finish. SAG support swaps bikes instantly at rest stops like Mack Cycle on Sunset Dr. Three fully stocked aid stations ensure seamless rejoining the peloton.

length.explanation: 100-mile route starts and ends at Four Seasons Hotel Miami, covering waterfront views through South Beach and Key Biscayne. Police-escorted peloton finishes by 11:10 AM after roughly 4 hours. Optional 75-mile distance available for slightly shorter commitment.

technicality.explanation: Fully paved roads with no gravel or technical demands, featuring smooth peloton rolling through Miami's Design District at Sweet Bird North rest stop. Police closures eliminate traffic interruptions on historic routes. Ideal for road bike handling without bike handling skills required.

elevation.explanation: Minimal 500 feet gain over 100 miles suits Florida's flat terrain from Brickell Ave start through Key Biscayne. No significant climbs disrupt the steady 18+ mph pace. Focus remains on endurance rather than vertical challenge.

climate.explanation: November timing in Miami delivers mild temperatures ideal for coastal riding along South Beach waterfront. Low humidity and waterfront breezes enhance comfort during the 7:00 AM start. Post-ride lunch at Four Seasons avoids midday heat buildup.

altitude.explanation: Sea-level riding throughout Miami, Key Biscayne, and South Beach routes keeps efforts aerobic. No altitude effects from Brickell Ave start to Design District rest stop. Pure flatland speed work in the peloton.

adventure.explanation: Police-escorted peloton adventure through Miami's scenic spots like Key Biscayne and South Beach, but structured with SAG and domestiques. Closed roads add thrill from Four Seasons Hotel to Mack Cycle stop. Charity focus with pro legends elevates beyond standard century.

prestige.explanation: Tour de France-style event draws Chris Froome and Alberto Contador for the 100-mile ride from Four Seasons Miami. Limited capacity around 1000 with yellow bibs for top fundraisers. Six-star charity ride with gala at Mana Wynwood cements notable status.

race_quality.explanation: Premium production includes pro domestiques, three rest stops at Mack Cycle and Sweet Bird North, and post-ride massages at Four Seasons. Police escort ensures fluid 18 mph minimum from 7:00 AM start. VIP mixer with pros night before adds elite polish.

experience.explanation: Unparalleled perks like limited-edition jersey, gourmet lunch, massages, and gala ticket at Mana Wynwood with celebrities. Meet pros like Froome at Friday mixer on Brickell Ave property. Two-night Four Seasons stay frames the full luxury weekend.

community.explanation: Rides alongside pro cyclists, athletes with IDD, and high-profile personalities in inclusive peloton from Miami start. Year-round training rides and social meet-ups build bonds pre-event. Fundraising for Best Buddies International unites diverse riders.

field_depth.explanation: Approximately 1000 participants including pros like Froome and Contador, but capped for exclusivity around Miami's 100-mile route. All abilities welcome in police-escorted group from Four Seasons. Focus on quality over massive field size.

value.explanation: Registration bundles hotel, jersey, bibs, gloves, gala access, and full support for 100-mile police-escorted ride through Key Biscayne. Three rest stops and SAG justify premium cost. Charity impact for IDD community adds meaningful return.

expenses.explanation: High entry fee covers luxury inclusions like Four Seasons stay and gala, but travel to Miami and fundraising minimums add up. Police escort and pro support elevate costs over standard centuries. Worth it for six-star charity experience.

cultural_impact.explanation: National charity ride raises funds for Best Buddies serving 200 million with IDD, highlighted by Miami Gala at Mana Wynwood with dignitaries. Pro involvement like Froome draws attention to inclusive cycling. Annual event since 2006 builds sustained awareness.

course_description.summary: Premium charity century ride starting and finishing at Four Seasons Hotel Miami on Brickell Ave, featuring a police-escorted peloton through South Beach, Key Biscayne, and waterfront views. 100-mile paved route maintains 18 mph minimum with SAG support and three rest stops. Pro legends like Chris Froome join all abilities including athletes with IDD.

key_segments.name: Start to First Rest Stop
key_segments.description: From Four Seasons Hotel Miami through initial waterfront to Mack Cycle on Sunset Dr.

key_segments.name: Design District Loop
key_segments.description: Peloton rolls to second stop at Sweet Bird North on NE 40th St amid urban scenic views.

key_segments.name: Key Biscayne Out-and-Back
key_segments.description: Historic coastal segment with beach views before return to Brickell finish.

course_description.elevation_profile: Flat Florida profile with ~500 ft total gain spread evenly; no major climbs.
course_description.aid_stations: Three fully stocked: 8:30 AM at Mack Cycle (5995 Sunset Dr.), 10:20 AM at Sweet Bird North (95 NE 40th St.), finish refreshments at Four Seasons.

travel.driving_from_major_city: Direct from MIA (15 min to Brickell); 4 hrs from Orlando
travel.accommodation: Complimentary 2 nights at Four Seasons Hotel Miami (1435 Brickell Ave)
travel.bike_transport: SAG provides instant bike swaps; fly with bike or ship to official shop

race_day.start_time: 7:00 AM sharp from Four Seasons Hotel Miami
race_day.cutoff: 11:10 AM finish; no strict cutoff due to escort
race_day.categories: 100-mile and 75-mile; all abilities, no competitive categories
race_day.awards: Yellow bibs for top 25 fundraisers; gala recognition

training_recommendations.minimum_fitness: Base of 2000 miles/year; comfortable at 18+ mph group pace for 4 hours
training_recommendations.ideal_preparation: Group rides mimicking 18 mph peloton; build to 100-mile efforts in final 8 weeks
training_recommendations.key_workouts: 3x weekly: 3-hr tempo rides, Saturday 60-80 mile group spins, strength for sustained power

history.notable_facts: Annual since 2006 supporting Best Buddies International; features pros like Froome, Contador; six-star charity benchmark with police-escorted centuries.

final_verdict.one_liner: Luxury charity peloton ride with pros through Miami's icons.
final_verdict.who_should_ride: Fundraisers seeking VIP treatment, pro encounters, and inclusive road centuries.
final_verdict.who_should_skip: Budget-conscious riders or those wanting rugged adventure or massive fields.
final_verdict.insider_tip: Max fundraising early for yellow bib; attend Friday pro mixer at Four Seasons for best networking.

suffering_zones.label: Peloton Pace Lock-In
suffering_zones.description: Sustaining 18+ mph for 4 hours in humid Miami peloton demands steady threshold power from Brickell to Key Biscayne.

suffering_zones.label: Florida Flat Fatigue
suffering_zones.description: Endless flat miles through South Beach test mental grit without climbs to break rhythm.

suffering_zones.label: Gala Endurance
suffering_zones.description: Post-ride recovery at Mana Wynwood gala till late night challenges full-day fueling.
